metadata.paper_title: Tokencake: A KV-Cache-centric Serving Framework for LLM-based Multi-Agent Applications

source_text.notes: Initial asset pass covers the motivation figures, scheduler mechanisms, and the core evaluation plots. Table 2 is kept as text evidence rather than a dense table crop.

assets.label: Figure 1
assets.title: Multi-Agent Workloads
assets.normalized_caption: Example LLM-based multi-agent applications, showing specialized agents and external function calls.
assets.raw_caption: Example LLM-based Multi-Agent Applications. Each colored box represents a specialized agent. Purple boxes denote function calls to external tools.
assets.notes: Tighter composite crop covering the two-panel workload diagram without surrounding paper text.

assets.label: Figure 2
assets.title: Space Contention
assets.normalized_caption: The space contention problem: critical inversions are frequent and non-critical agents can hold KV-cache blocks.
assets.notes: Composite crop of both subpanels for the space-contention evidence.

assets.label: Figure 3
assets.title: Time Underutilization
assets.normalized_caption: The time underutilization problem: stalled agents leave idle KV-cache blocks in GPU memory during function calls.
assets.notes: Composite crop of both subpanels for the time-underutilization evidence.

assets.label: Figure 4
assets.title: Tokencake Overview
assets.normalized_caption: Tokencake architecture overview with the Frontend API, Space Scheduler, and Time Scheduler.
assets.notes: Primary architecture figure for the overall system story.

assets.label: Figure 6
assets.title: Scheduler Coordination
assets.normalized_caption: Coordination between the Space Scheduler and the Time Scheduler.
assets.raw_caption: Coordination between the Space Scheduler and the Time Scheduler.
assets.source_section: 3.2 Coordination between Space and Time Schedulers
assets.notes: Useful companion asset when the overview slide also needs the control interaction.

assets.label: Figure 7
assets.title: Time Scheduler Lifecycle
assets.normalized_caption: Lifecycle of the Time Scheduler's offload and predictive upload mechanism.
assets.raw_caption: Lifecycle of the Time Scheduler's offload and predictive upload mechanism.
assets.notes: Main mechanism figure for the time scheduler.

assets.label: Figure 8
assets.title: Dynamic Memory Partitioning
assets.normalized_caption: The Space Scheduler's dynamic memory partitioning feedback loop.
assets.raw_caption: The Space Scheduler's dynamic memory partitioning feedback loop.
assets.source_section: 5.1 Runtime Control with Dynamic Memory Partitioning
assets.notes: Main mechanism figure for the space scheduler.

assets.label: Figure 9
assets.title: End-to-End Latency
assets.normalized_caption: End-to-end latency comparison of Tokencake, vLLM, and LightLLM across applications, models, and request rates.
assets.raw_caption: End-to-end application latency comparison of Tokencake, vLLM, and LightLLM. Each chart plots average latency against queries-per-second (QPS) for the specified application, model, and dataset.

assets.label: Figure 10
assets.title: GPU KV Utilization
assets.normalized_caption: GPU KV-cache utilization under varying load.
assets.raw_caption: GPU KV Cache utilization under varying load.
assets.notes: Supports the argument that latency gains come from better memory use.

assets.label: Figure 11
assets.title: Agent-Type Latency
assets.notes: Per-agent evidence that Tokencake improves individual stages.

assets.label: Figure 12
assets.title: Abnormal Agents
assets.normalized_caption: Reduction in the count of abnormal agents, defined as agents whose execution time exceeds 1.5x the average for their type.
assets.raw_caption: Reduction in the count of abnormal agents. An agent is considered abnormal if its execution time exceeds 1.5x the average for its type.
assets.notes: Compact evidence that the scheduler reduces harmful outliers on the critical path.

assets.label: Figure 13
assets.title: Offload vs Recompute
assets.normalized_caption: Time tradeoff between KV-cache reuse through offload/upload and full recomputation.
assets.raw_caption: Time tradeoff between KV Cache reuse and recomputation.
assets.notes: Shows why offloading is worthwhile for stalled agents.

assets.label: Figure 14
assets.title: Overhead Mitigation
assets.normalized_caption: Overhead mitigation for KV-cache offload and upload operations.
assets.raw_caption: Overhead Mitigation for KV Cache Offload and Upload Operations.
assets.notes: Microbenchmark showing why Tokencake's offload optimizations matter.

notes: Figure registry prepared before briefing and slide planning.
